steps.title: Find the side length
steps.content: A cube with side length $s$ has surface area $6s^2$. The given surface area is $6\left(\frac{k}{4}\right)^2$, so $$6s^2=6\left(\frac{k}{4}\right)^2$$ $$s=\frac{k}{4}$$

steps.title: Find one face perimeter
steps.content: One face is a square with side length $\frac{k}{4}$, so its perimeter is $$4\left(\frac{k}{4}\right)=k$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice B (‘$k$’) is $k$, so B is correct.
